observations.recommendation: 2. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the personnel concerned to submit the Solid Waste Management Plan, pursuant to Section 16 of the Republic Act (RA) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991.

3. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Supply Officer to submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) in accordance with Section 124 (2) of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting System (PPSAS) 17 – Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE).

6. We recommend to the Management to instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Property, Plant and Equipment, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government Units, Volume I and Section 13 and 45 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government Units, Volume II.

1.4.1 Request the depository bank the copies of Bank Statements (BS) including debit/credit memos (DM/CM), paid checks, etc., within five (5) days after the end of each month, in accordance with Section 3.1 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
1.4.2 Prepare and submit the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements and original copies of debit/credit memos within the reglementary period in pursuant to Section 74 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

7. Hence, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant direct the concerned staff to prepare and maintain schedules and Subsidiary Ledgers for Payable accounts pursuant to sections 111 (1) and 114 (2) of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445 or otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

5.4.1 Submit all perfected contracts, purchase orders, and all the supporting documentation within the prescribed period of five working days from the date of its issuance to afford the Auditor time to make an initial review of the contracts and purchase orders to identify to management any defects and/or deficiencies, if any, as provided under Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate timely review and appropriate action by the Auditor, as well as the Agency.
Request for technical inspection and review of contracts by the COA Technical Audit Specialist (TAS) as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001.

6.9.1 Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory of all Property, Plant and Equipment of the Municipality of Mangudadatu, Maguindanao and as well as the preparation and submission of the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) in accordance with MNGAS for LGUs to be submitted to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.
6.9.2 General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to maintain their respective Property Cards. Subsequently, reconcile their record, update each property ledger cards and record/reflect the correct valuation of PPE account in the Statement of Financial Position of the current year.

12. In view of the foregoing, we recommend the management that at the start of the year, submit the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year with complete details and exercised full disclosure policy as required by Sections 1.1 and 2.1 of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

8.5.1 Require the Municipal Engineer to accomplish the necessary Status of Work Accomplished for every infrastructure project implemented by management, which will be validated by the Municipality’s Inspectorate Committee and will be the basis for the issuance of the related Certificates of Completion and Acceptance by the head of agency.
8.5.2 Require the submission to the Office of the Auditor of the Reports on Utilization, Status of Work Accomplished, Inspection Reports, and Certificates of Completion and Acceptance on Projects / Programs under the 20% Development Fund, pursuant to Section 2.1 of DILG – DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017 and Appendix 1 of the RIRR of RA 9184, respectively.

1.7 We recommended that the LCE to instruct the Municipal Accountant
to:
1.7.1 Request copies of bank statements (BS), including debit/credit
memos (DM/CM), cashed checks, etc., from the depository bank
not later than five (5) days following the end of each month in
accordance with Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated
October 2, 1996,
1.7.2 Comply with Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011
dated October 2, 1996 and Section 74 of PD 1445 by preparing
and submitting the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements
and original copies of debit/credit memos within the required
time frame.

2.5 Hence, we recommended that the LCE:
2.5.1 Instruct the municipal accountant to make comparable prior
years' adjustments in the books that will be closed to the
Government Equity Account; and
2.5.2 Ensure that the Municipal Accountant recognizes depreciation
expense on all PPE accounts.

3.9 In light of the aforementioned, we recommended that the LCE instruct:
3.9.1 The Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of all the
LGU’s PPE and prepare the corresponding RPCPPE, which
must be submitted to the Office of the Auditor by January 31 of
each year, pursuant to Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs
Volume I.
3.9.2 The municipal accountant and general services officer to keep
track of their individual property cards. Afterwards, reconcile
their records, amend each property ledger card, and reflect the
right PPE account valuation in the current year's Statement of
Financial Position, in accordance with Sections 119 and 120 of
20 the MNGAS for LGUs Volume I and Sections 13 and 45 of the
same Manual Volume II.

4.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant classify to the Other
Maintenance and Operating Expenses Account in compliance with
paragraph 106 of the IPSAS 1-Presentation of Financial Statements
and Section 61 of PD 1445, and submit to the Office of the Auditor the
breakdown of expenditures that were charged to Other Maintenance
and Operating Expenses during the current year.

5.6 We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to
submit a Revised Notes to Financial Statements for CY 2022 that
incorporates the detailed analyses of amounts as shown in the
Statement of Changes in Net Assets/Equity specifically the Adjustment
of Net Revenue recognized directly in Net Assets/Equity in accordance
with Section 109 of PD 1445 and Section 80 of the MNGAS for LGUs,
Volume I.

6.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the financial
statements together with the Statement of Comparison of Budget and
Actual Amounts which is a part of the financial statements, in
accordance with Section 100 and Section 111 (1) of PD 1445.

7.6 Given what has been said this far, we recommended that the LCE:
7.6.1 Demand the Municipal Engineer to complete the required
Status of Work Accomplished for each infrastructure project
that management implements. This document will be verified by
the Municipality's Inspectorate Committee and serve as the
basis for the issuance of the corresponding Certificates of
Completion and Acceptance by the head of agency.
7.6.2 Require that the Reports on Utilization, Status of Work
Accomplished, Inspection Reports, and Certificates of
Completion and Acceptance on Projects / Programs under the
20% Development Fund be submitted to the Office of the
Auditor, in accordance with Section 2.1 of the DILG-DBM JMC
No. 2017 and Appendix 1 of the RIRR of RA 9184,
respectively.

8.4 As a result, we recommended that the Management:
8.4.1 Submit all perfected contracts, purchase orders and all
supporting documentation within the allotted time frame of five
working days from the date of its issuance. This will allow the
Auditor time to review the contracts and purchase orders and
identify to management any defects and/or deficiencies, if any,
in accordance with Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
8.4.2 Submit the contract for technical inspection and evaluation by
the COA Technical Audit Specialist (TAS), in accordance with
COA Circular No. 2009-001.

1. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) of the Municipal Government of Mangudadatu, Maguindanao to instruct the Municipal Development Coordinator (MDPC) to submit the Comprehensive Land Use Plan (CLUP), Comprehensive Development Plan (CDP), Legislative Agenda (ELA) and Local Development Investment Program (LDIP) in accordance with Section 106 of the Republic Act (RA) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991.

4. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct Municipal Accountant to depreciate Properties, Plant and Equipment (PPE) and record some PPE excluded in the Notes to Financial Statements, and the Inventory taking Committee to submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), in accordance with Sections 262 and 380 of the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for Local Government Units, Volume I and Section 124 (2) of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting System (PPSAS) 17 – Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE).

3.4.2 Practice obtaining complete documentation as enumerated in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2021, before certifying propriety of the claims/disbursements of government funds.

14. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit a Revised Notes to Financial Statements for CY 2021 incorporating the detailed analyses of amounts shown on the face of the Statement of Financial Position, Statement of Financial Performance and Statement of Cash Flows, in accordance with Section 109 of the Presidential Decree (PD) 1445 and Section 80 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.

15. Hence, we recommend the management to immediately comply with the submission of the quarterly and semestral reports, in accordance with Section 122 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

(PD) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of

2.5 Hence, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant direct the
concerned staff to prepare and maintain schedules and Subsidiary
Ledgers for Payable accounts pursuant to sections 111 (1) and 114 (2)
of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445 or otherwise known as the
Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

3.4 Thus, we recommend the Municipal Accountant to comply with
Section 4 (6) and 39 of P.D. No. 1445 and other applicable provisions
of laws, rules and regulations by:
3.4.1 Providing the Office of the Auditor copies complete
documentary requirements such as the checks issued and other
supporting documents, in accordance with Commission on
Audit (COA) Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, for
the month of December 2021.
3.4.2 Practice obtaining complete documentation as enumerated in
COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2021, before
certifying propriety of the claims/disbursements of government
funds.

4.9 In view of the foregoing, we recommend the management to:
4.9.1 Immediately comply with the submission of the documents and
reports mentioned;
4.9.2 Impose the suspension of payment of salaries of the
Accountable Officers who repeatedly failed to submit the documents
and reports mentioned above within the prescribed period, as
mandated in Section 122 of P.D. No. 1445; and
4.9.3 Be mindful, on pain of an administrative disciplinary action,
under Section 127 of P.D. No. 1445 which states that unjustified
failure of a public officer to comply with any of the provision of PD
No. 1445 shall constitute neglect of duty.

5.4 Therefore, we recommend the management to:
5.4.1 Submit all perfected contracts, purchase orders, and all the
supporting documents within the prescribed period of five
working days from the date of its issuance to afford the
Auditor time to make an initial review of the contracts and
purchase orders to identify to management any defects and/or
deficiencies, if any, as provided under Section 3 of COA
Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate timely review and
appropriate action by the Auditor, as well as the Agency.
5.4.2 Request for technical inspection and review of contracts by the
COA Technical Audit Specialist (TAS) as required under COA
Circular No. 2009-001.

6.9 In relation to the foregoing, we recommend the Local Chief Executive
(LCE) to direct the:
6.9.1 Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory of all
Property, Plant and Equipment of the Municipality of
Mangudadatu, Maguindanao and as well as the preparation of
the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RCPPE), in accordance with MNGAS for LGUs to
be submitted to the Office of the Auditor not later than
January 31 of each year.
6.9.2 General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to
maintain their respective Property Cards. Subsequently,
reconcile their record, update each property ledger cards and
record/reflect the correct valuation of PPE account in the
Statement of Financial Position of the current year.

7.4 Hence, we recommend the management that at the start of the year,
submit the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to
be implemented during the year with complete details and exercised
full disclosure policy as required by Sections 1.1 and 2.1 of
Commission on Audit (COA) Circular 2013-004 dated January 30,
2013.

8.5 In view of the foregoing, we recommend the Local Chief Executive
(LCE) to:
8.5.1 Require the Municipal Engineer to accomplish the necessary
Status of Work Accomplished for every infrastructure project
implemented by management, which will be validated by the
Municipality’s Inspectorate Committee and will be the basis for the
issuance of the related Certificates of Completion and Acceptance by
the head of agency.
8.5.2 Require the submission to the Office of the Auditor of the
Reports on Utilization, Status of Work Accomplished,
Inspection Reports, and Certificates of Completion and
Acceptance on Projects / Programs under the 20%
Development Fund, pursuant to Section 2.1 of DILG – DBM
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017 and Appendix 1 of the
Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act
No. 9184, respectively.

9.4 We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the
Municipal Accountant to submit a Revised Notes to Financial
Statements for CY 2021 incorporating the detailed analyses of
amounts shown on the face of the Statement of Financial Position,
Statement of Financial Performance and Statement of Cash Flows, in
accordance with Section 109 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) 1445 and
Section 80 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System
for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.

10.4 Hence, we recommend the management to immediately comply with
the submission of the quarterly and semestral reports, in accordance
with Section 122 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise
known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

observations.managementAction: 1. The management could not present the Municipal plan. Funding was these submission would be made later upon completion of the problem.
observations.reason: Due to being a Non – IRA Municipality, however, it performs the functions of a regular municipality where the basic services were provided to its constituents on Health, Sanitation, Education and Safety. Only in the CY 2017 that the management had prepared these development plans that would be applicable for the coming year.

observations.recommendation: 2. We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal Government of Mangudadatu to submit the Solid Waste Management Plan, pursuant to Section 16 of the Republic Act (RA) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.managementAction: 2. No Solid Waste Management Plan for CY 2016 was prepared by the management. But instead, a campaign on the Solid Waste Management was conducted on every barangays of the Municipality. A Solid Waste segregation was emphasized by the management for feasible re – use, recycling and composting programs.
observations.reason: No budget for the purpose. Still accumulating funds since it is a NON-IRA Municipality.

1. Require collecting officials e mployee to remit daily collections to the bank deposit daily if practical and on the following day, if it’s not to the authorized government depository bank.

observations.recommendation: 3. Require the management to install sound system of internal control, specifically in the cash section, pursuant to Section 124, PD 1445.

4. We recommend that management strictly implement sound and effective internal control and such other measures to ensure prompt reporting, remittances and deposit of collect in order to reflect the correct cash balances in the financial statements. Each business day and remittances of collections daily, especially when collections exceed ₱ 5,000.00.
